codeReview.summary: The streamlit_app.py rewrite is well-executed. The worker replaced all Plotly chart code (NAV chart, drawdown chart, monthly heatmap, per-stock bar chart) with a clean two-tab layout using QuantStats tearsheet and backtesting.py Bokeh charts, both embedded via st.components.v1.html(). The 6 metric cards (Total Return, CAGR, Sharpe, Sortino, Max Drawdown, Win Rate) are correctly implemented. Tab 2 correctly filters stocks with trades and runs per-stock backtests with Bokeh visualization. Session state caching for backtest results is preserved. All 177 tests pass. Only file changed was streamlit_app.py (193 insertions, 321 deletions). No off-limits files (src/strategy/, src/backtest/, src/data_layer/) were modified. Two non-blocking issues identified: the welcome message still references old Plotly features that no longer exist, and CAGR calculation has a theoretical edge case with extreme losses.

issues.description: Welcome message still references old Plotly-based features that were removed: 'Interactive NAV Chart: Compare strategy performance against CSI 300 and ChiNext benchmarks', 'Drawdown Chart: Visualize underwater periods', 'Monthly Returns Heatmap: See seasonality patterns', 'Per-Stock Performance: Identify top performers'. Should be updated to reference the new two-tab layout with QuantStats tearsheet and Bokeh charts.

CAGR calculation: `(1 + metrics['profit_total']) ** (1 / years) - 1` will fail if profit_total < -1 (total loss exceeds 100%), as it would attempt to raise a negative number to a fractional power. This is theoretically possible if the backtest engine allows negative NAV. Adding a guard like `max(0, 1 + metrics['profit_total'])` would be safer.

stats_dict.get('Sharpe Ratio', 0.0) defaults to 0.0, but backtesting.py can return NaN for Sharpe Ratio when there are insufficient trades. Formatting NaN with f'{sharpe:.3f}' works but displays 'nan'. Same applies to 'Max. Drawdown [%]' and 'Return [%]'. Consider handling NaN gracefully with a display fallback.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The welcome message (shown before any backtest is run) still references the old Plotly-based feature list (NAV Chart, Drawdown Chart, Monthly Returns Heatmap, Per-Stock Performance bar chart). This was part of the old code that the worker did not update to match the new two-tab layout. AGENTS.md says 'No custom Plotly charts' but doesn't explicitly require updating the welcome text. The welcome message is cosmetic but misleads users about what the app actually does.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: quant-dashboard/streamlit_app.py lines 372-395: Welcome markdown still lists '📈 Interactive NAV Chart', '📉 Drawdown Chart', '🔥 Monthly Returns Heatmap', '🏆 Per-Stock Performance' — all of which were removed in this commit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md in .factory/library/ still documents the old Plotly-based architecture ('Plotly for charts: All interactive charts (NAV, drawdown, heatmap, bar) use plotly.graph_objects via st.plotly_chart()') and old project structure. It was not updated to reflect the new two-tab architecture with QuantStats and Bokeh. This is a documentation drift concern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md Key Design Decision #3: 'Plotly for charts: All interactive charts (NAV, drawdown, heatmap, bar) use plotly.graph_objects via st.plotly_chart().' — this is no longer true after the dashboard rewrite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: services.yaml uses port 8020 for Streamlit but AGENTS.md says 'Port Range: 8501 (Streamlit default)'. The validation contract VAL-DASH-001 checks localhost:8501. This discrepancy exists from before this feature but may cause confusion during validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml shows '--server.port 8020' while AGENTS.md says 'Port Range: 8501 (Streamlit default)' and VAL-DASH-001 references 'localhost:8501'.

summary: PASS. The dashboard rewrite is solid — all Plotly code removed, two-tab layout implemented correctly with QuantStats tearsheet (Tab 1) and Bokeh charts (Tab 2), 6 metric cards present, session state caching preserved, all 177 tests pass, and no off-limits files were modified. Three non-blocking issues found: (1) welcome message still references old removed features, (2) CAGR edge case with extreme losses, (3) potential NaN display in per-stock metrics. The implementation faithfully follows the feature description and AGENTS.md conventions.
